page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-192GB is a KVM-hypervised virtual server featuring 64 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 3.0 GB per core. The instance is powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is shared, and the hardware configuration does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPUs.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server exhibits poor single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the bottom 10%, and weak text processing in Geekbench. However, multi-core execution is robust, with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Passmark CPU scores, and Redis SET operations all performing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier.

The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, predictable single-core performance. However, the high physical core count and strong multi-threaded capabilities make this server efficient for highly parallelized tasks. It is suitable for multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and parallelized general-purpose workloads that can tolerate shared CPU resources.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-192GB is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 64 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM, running on a KVM hypervisor. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, this instance lacks bundled local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark data indicates a clear tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, ranking in the bottom 10% of stress-ng tests, whereas multi-core performance is highly competitive, with Passmark CPU metrics and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 25%. Additionally, Redis SET operations demonstrate strong throughput. Given the shared resource model and high core density, this server is best utilized for highly parallelized, non-latency-sensitive workloads such as multi-threaded data processing, database operations, and general-purpose batch computing.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-192GB is a KVM virtual server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-core performance alongside shared CPU allocation and no local storage.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-192GB features 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance with shared CPU allocation.
tagline: A high-core virtual server with shared AMD EPYC processors and 192 GB RAM, optimized for parallelized multi-threaded workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory (3.0 GB per core)
Strong multi-core performance in Passmark and bzip3 benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU resources
Suited for parallelized batch processing and database operations
